mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment growth signals an imminent economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and consumer credit growth indicate a synchronized withdrawal of household spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when housing starts and credit growth diverge, or forward returns show no significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy aggregate supply, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above 105, or the signal loses predictive power during supply-side shocks (e.g., oil price spikes > 20% in 3 months).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds a 3.0% annualized inflation threshold, real yields rise and purchasing power erosion triggers equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPI annualized growth exceeding 3.0%, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.5% signals robust economic momentum, which lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in high-GDP quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payroll growth and a stable unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market without wage-push inflation, which sustains equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails when payroll growth exceeds 200k but unemployment rises, or forward returns show no significant difference between triggered and non-triggered periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, higher borrowing costs compress corporate leverage and discount future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
